delta.description: Banded shoulder external rotation prehab drill. Elbow pinned to ribs, forearm rotates outward against band resistance to load the infraspinatus and teres minor before pressing work.
steps: Anchor band at elbow height; grip free end in working hand.
Elbow pinned to side at 90°, forearm across belly to start.
Rotate forearm outward, keeping elbow glued to the side.
Pause at end range, control return.
Complete reps, switch sides.
tips: Light band — prehab dose, not a strength lift.
Elbow stays glued to side — no shoulder abduction.
Slow tempo both ways; full ROM each rep.
Pre-pressing days, before warm-up sets.

delta.description: Loaded spinal-flexion crunch. Kneeling under a high cable, the abs curl the torso downward against the rope's pull for top-down ab loading without hip-flexor dominance.
Cable flye performed from low pulleys upward to an above-eye-line meeting point. Biases clavicular (upper-chest) fibers — the visible shape under existing tissue.

steps: Attach ankle cuff to a low pulley; cuff on the far ankle.
Stand sideways to the stack, near hand on upright for balance.
Working leg slightly behind the line of the body, knee soft.
Raise straight leg out to the side, squeezing glute medius at top.
Control return — don't let the stack slam.
tips: Tiny lean AWAY from the stack to free hip ROM.
Glute medius drives the lift — not lower-back side-bend.
Standing knee soft, not hyperextended.
High reps, light load — this is side-glute hypertrophy, not a power lift.
Hinge the standing leg slightly forward for hip extension ROM.
Squeeze glute at end range — pause before reversing.
Slow eccentric; don't let the stack slam back.
No lower-back arch to gain ROM — ribs over hips.

delta.description: Neutral-grip cable curl with a rope handle. Brachialis-biased — the under-bicep muscle that pushes the biceps up and out, driving arm width from all angles.

tips: Full ROM — deep stretch at bottom, full plantar flexion at top.
Pause 1 sec at peak contraction.
Standing/straight-knee biases gastrocnemius (the visible diamond).
Slow eccentric; calves grow from time under stretch.

delta.description: Seated calf raise. Bent-knee position takes gastrocnemius off the line and isolates the soleus — the deep, slow-twitch calf below the gastroc.
Chest-supported row using a machine or T-bar with a chest pad. The pad eliminates torso cheat so rhomboids and mid-traps fire instead of lower-back compensation.

steps: Side-lying, hips and knees bent ~90°, band around knees.
Stack the heels together; feet stay touching.
Open the top knee against the band by rotating from the hip.
Pause at end range, control return — don't let the band snap back.
Complete reps, flip sides.
tips: Glute medius activator — wakes side-glute before lower-day work.
Don't roll the pelvis back — keep hips stacked vertically.
Light band; this is activation, not strength.
10-15 reps each side, pre-Lower A.

delta.description: Donkey calf raise. Bent-over with hips hinged forward, partner or hip-belt loading the lower back. Hip flexion lengthens the gastrocnemius for a stretch-biased calf lift.
steps: Hinge at hips and rest forearms on a stable surface, back parallel to floor.
Balls of feet on a raised platform, heels free.
Partner sits across lower back, OR use a hip-belt loaded.
Press up to full plantar flexion, hold 1 sec.
Lower under control to a deep stretch at bottom.
tips: Hip flexion stretches the gastroc — strongest stretch position for calves.
Slow tempo — calves respond to time under tension.
No partner? Sub with leg-press calf raise.
Pause at peak contraction; full ROM both ways.

delta.description: Single-leg quad-and-glute lift with rear foot elevated on a bench. The split position loads the front leg in deep hip flexion — large hypertrophy stimulus per side.
steps: Stand 2-3 ft in front of a bench, DBs in each hand.
Top of rear foot on bench, laces down.
Front foot far enough out that front shin stays vertical at the bottom.
Descend by bending front knee and hip; rear knee tracks down toward floor.
Drive up through the front-foot midfoot; lock out at top.
Complete reps, switch legs.
tips: Vertical front shin = glute bias. Knee over toe = quad bias.
Don't push off the rear foot — it's just balance.
Torso forward = glute bias; upright = quad bias.
Goblet variation works fine if grip fails before legs.

delta.description: Neutral-grip dumbbell curl, palms facing each other through the full ROM. Brachialis-biased — drives arm width by pushing the biceps up and out.
steps: DBs by sides, palms facing each other (neutral grip).
Elbows pinned to ribs.
Curl one or both DBs to shoulder height, keeping palms neutral throughout.
Pause briefly at top.
Control descent to full extension.
tips: Neutral grip = brachialis bias (the under-arm width pusher).
Elbows do NOT drift forward — that's a front-delt cheat.
Slow eccentric; stretch position is where the growth lives.
Both arms together for density; alternate if grip fails first.

delta.description: Unilateral DB row with one knee on a bench. The chest-supported torso lets the lat work through full ROM without lower-back compensation.
steps: Same-side knee + hand on a flat bench, torso parallel to floor.
Working hand holds DB, arm fully extended toward floor.
Pull DB to hip — elbow drives back and up.
Pause at top with shoulder blade retracted.
Control descent to full stretch.
Complete reps, switch sides.
tips: Lead with the elbow, not the hand — biceps shouldn't run the lift.
Don't rotate the torso to gain ROM; shoulders stay square.
Full stretch at the bottom — let the shoulder blade protract.
Cue: pull the elbow to the hip.

delta.description: Single-leg step-up to a box or bench with DBs in hand. Glute- or quad-biased depending on box height and torso angle.
steps: DBs in each hand at sides, stand facing a knee-height box.
Place working foot fully on the box.
Drive through the front midfoot to stand tall on the box.
Trailing leg comes up to balance — don't push off the floor.
Step down with the trailing leg first, soft landing.
Complete reps one side, switch.
tips: Higher box + slight forward lean = glute bias.
Lower box + upright torso = quad bias.
Push through the BOX, not off the floor.
Slow descent; the eccentric stimulus is half the lift.

delta.description: Bent-over DB raise with arms forming a Y. Loads lower-trap and rear-delt fibers in their primary line of pull — posture-fixer without upper-trap creep.
steps: Hinge at hips ~45°, light DBs in each hand, palms facing each other.
Arms slightly bent, hanging straight down at start.
Raise both arms out and slightly forward to form a Y, thumbs leading.
Stop at shoulder-line height; don't shrug.
Control descent — slow eccentric.
tips: LIGHT DBs — this is posture work, not a strength lift.
Thumbs UP through the raise; thumbs forward is the wrong cue.
No traps/shrug — if upper-trap kicks in, drop the weight.
Pairs with face pulls + rear delts for posture trio.
Rope at eye height; pull the rope to the FACE not the chest.
Lead with the elbows — flare them outward to bias rear delt.
Squeeze rear delts at peak contraction.
If upper traps fire instead, drop the weight and re-cue elbows.

delta.description: Barbell hip thrust. Upper back on a bench, barbell across hips, drive hips up to full extension — the most direct glute-builder in a gym setting.
steps: Sit on floor with upper-back against the long edge of a bench.
Roll the barbell into the hip crease (use a pad).
Feet flat ~shoulder-width, shins vertical at the top of the rep.
Drive through heels to lift hips until thighs are parallel to floor.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top, pause 1 sec.
Lower under control to a deep stretch.
tips: Top position: knees, hips, shoulders form a straight line.
Chin tucked, ribs DOWN — don't hyperextend the lumbar.
Drive through the HEELS — toes-up cue helps.
Full ROM both ways: deep stretch at bottom, lockout squeeze at top.

steps: Set up a standard barbell hip thrust (back on bench, bar at hip crease).
Band around knees, tension active before unracking.
Press hips up to lockout with knees pushing OUT against the band.
At top, perform 3 small pulses keeping knees apart.
Lower under control to start; that's one rep.
tips: Knees push OUT against the band the whole rep — glute medius co-fires.
Pulses are small — don't drop and re-press.
Squeeze every rep; this is glute-finisher work, not strength.
Best paired with a heavy hip-thrust top set; pulse as the burnout.

delta.description: Reclined incline DB curl with bench at 60°. The deep stretch puts the long head of the biceps in its most lengthened position — stretch-bias hypertrophy.
Low-incline DB press at 30°. Bias is clavicular (upper-chest) fibers — the visible shape just under the collarbone, not chest mass.

tips: Roll pad sits on the Achilles, not mid-calf.
Lying/straight-hip biases the SHORT head of hamstrings.
Slow eccentric — heels return to full knee extension.
Pause at peak contraction; don't slam reps.
One leg at a time — better mind-muscle than alternating quickly.
Don't arch the lower back to gain ROM; ribs over hips.
Slow descent; the eccentric is where the stimulus lives.
Pause at peak contraction; squeeze hamstring at top.
Foot position high = glute/ham bias; low = quad bias.
Don't lock out the knees at the top.
Lower to ~90° knee bend — don't bounce out of the hole.
Keep lower back pressed into the seat — no butt lift.

delta.description: Side-lying lateral raise on a 30° incline bench. The bottom of the rep loads the medial delt in its fully stretched position — pure stretch-bias dose.
Loaded crunch on a machine. Holding handles or pad while curling the spine flexes the abs against a fixed resistance line — no hip-flexor compensation.
steps: Adjust machine seat so chest pad sits at sternum height.
Grip handles or hold pad, abs braced.
Curl spine downward — bring sternum toward pelvis.
Pause at peak abdominal contraction.
Control return to full stretch position.
tips: Flex the SPINE, not the hips — chase the ab contraction.
Arms just hold position; don't pull with them.
Slow eccentric — full stretch at top of the lift.
Cap the load: heavy crunches risk upper-ab thickening; chase reps.

delta.description: Cable triceps extension with arms overhead, rope attachment. The overhead position puts the long head of the triceps in deep stretch — stretch-mediated hypertrophy.
Anti-rotation core press with a band. Standing perpendicular to anchor, the press extends a band away from chest while the core resists rotational pull.
Standing band rotation. Arms extended, torso rotates against a band-anchored line — a rotational core strength move (note: opposite of Pallof PRESS, which is anti-rotation).
steps: Anchor band at chest height, stand perpendicular to anchor.
Both hands hold band straight out from sternum.
Rotate torso AWAY from the anchor — hands stay in front of chest.
Pause at end range; control return.
Complete reps both sides.
tips: Confusingly named: PRESS = anti-rotation, ROTATIONS = actual rotation.
Hips stay square — rotation comes from thoracic spine.
Light band — torso control over force.
WHR-priority? Prefer Pallof PRESS; loaded rotations add waist thickness.
Lean back ~10°; don't pull bar behind the head.
Lead with elbows down and back — bar to upper chest.
Squeeze lats at the bottom; pause briefly.
Full stretch at top; let shoulder blades rise.
Wide grip = lat width bias; shoulder-width = mid-back.

delta.description: Bent-over DB rear delt fly. The hinge position aligns the rear delts with gravity through the raise — direct rear-delt isolator.
steps: Hinge at hips ~45°, light DBs in each hand, arms hanging straight down.
Slight bend in elbows, palms facing each other.
Raise DBs out laterally to shoulder line — squeeze rear delts.
Pause at top; don't shrug.
Control descent to full stretch at bottom.
tips: Lead with the ELBOWS, not the hands — knuckles point sideways.
Light DBs — easy to compensate with traps if heavy.
Squeeze at the top with rear delts, not shoulder blades.
Don't extend the elbows during the raise — fixed slight bend.

delta.description: Lying reverse crunch. Pelvis curls toward ribs through a small posterior pelvic tilt — lower-ab biased without hip-flexor dominance or anterior-tilt risk.
Reverse pec deck. Chest against the pad, arms pull handles back and out by squeezing shoulder blades — rear-delt and mid-trap loader without torso cheat.
Seated cable row from a low pulley. Wide-handle or V-grip — primary lat and mid-back work with elbows tucked into the body.
steps: Sit at the cable row station, feet on platform, slight knee bend.
Grip handle, torso upright, arms fully extended at start.
Pull handle to lower ribs, leading with the elbows.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at peak — pause briefly.
Control return to full stretch; don't slump forward.
tips: Torso angle stays steady — no rocking to add ROM.
Elbows tucked tight = mid-back bias; flared = rear delt bias.
Full stretch at the front; full retraction at the back.
Pause and squeeze at peak — chase contraction, not range.

delta.description: Seated machine leg curl. Hip-flexed seated position lengthens the hamstrings at the start — biases the long head and adds stretch-mediated stimulus.

steps: Stand on one leg, slight knee bend; DB in opposite hand (or bodyweight).
Hinge at hip — torso tips forward as rear leg extends straight back.
Vertical shin on the working leg; back stays neutral.
Lower until torso ~parallel to floor or hamstring stretch limits ROM.
Drive through standing-leg heel to return to standing.
Complete reps, switch legs.
tips: Vertical shin cue = pure hip hinge, glute/ham loaded.
Cue: close a car door behind you with the back foot — keeps hips square.
Bodyweight first; use mirror to verify shin and back position.
Both hip points stay level — don't rotate toward lifting leg.
Elbows pinned to ribs — no shoulder flexion.
Split the rope at the bottom for full triceps contraction.
Slow eccentric — control the return to full stretch.
If shoulders kick in, the weight is too heavy.

delta.description: Wide-grip EZ-bar curl. Palms angled outward emphasizes the short (inner) head of the biceps — drives arm width on the front view.
